title: ranunculus palustris subsp. procerus
intro: Ranunculus palustris subsp. procerus is a subspecies of the lesser celandine plant, a perennial herb native to Europe. The plant's medicinal properties include its ability to act as a purgative and emetic, due to the presence of toxic compounds such as protoanemonin and ranunculin, which should be handled with caution. The health benefits of Ranunculus palustris subsp. procerus are largely anecdotal, but some sources suggest it may have anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, although further research is needed to confirm this. The plant is sometimes used in herbal preparations such as tinctures and infusions, but it's essential to note that it requires careful handling and dosing due to its toxicity.
